method: Read all 8 source files completely + geometry.py; imported weapons.py (runs the import-time bake loop) to enumerate all 53 records programmatically; grep-verified every w['gap']/geo['gap']/w['head']/sel_head/reach_base call site; walked wrapper.engagement() beat-by-beat. Extends the 5 prior fable lenses to full coverage rather than re-deriving them.

element_mode_inventory.note: simple single-element blade; mode question moot

3 base elements (hammer/beak/spike) 1:1 with 3 mode_elements BY LIST ORDER + comment only; mode_elements carry NO x_m/extent (keys are exactly ['geo','head']). whole_gap=0.78 but per-mode point gaps are 0.59 and 0.82 — comparator + core.strike read whole 0.78 for both.
3 base / 3 mode by list order; 2 dual_role guards (cross-blades) shadow the 2 curved_cut mode_elements at same x_m; whole_gap=0.70 vs point mode_element gap=0.75 — comparator reads whole.

SILENT-ZIP-FAILURE class: 3 mass elements (center prong + 2 flank tines) but NO mode_elements — _mode_elements() collapses to ONE synthesized {head=native 'point', geo=whole}. The 2 flank-tine elements + 2 dual_role catch-guards never surface as selectable modes. Extra mass elements are mass-model only.
single element; has adornments (tassel) + wing-lug guards (non-dual-role here)

SILENT-ZIP-FAILURE: 3 mass elements (central point + 2 side prongs) + 2 dual_role guards, but NO mode_elements — collapses to 1 synthesized {head='cut_thrust',geo=whole}. Side-prong bind-catch elements invisible to select_mode.
SILENT-ZIP-FAILURE: 3 mass elements (ox-tongue + 2 wing-lugs) + 2 dual_role guards, NO mode_elements. Wing-lug catch elements never a selectable mode.

SILENT-ZIP-FAILURE: 2 mass elements (dao blade + rear spike/hook notch) + 1 dual_role guard, NO mode_elements. Rear hook/spike element (an explicit hook per its own comment) never becomes a mode; native head 'curved_cut' only.
SILENT-ZIP-FAILURE: 2 mass elements (fauchard blade + back-hook spike) + 1 dual_role guard, NO mode_elements. Back-hook element invisible to select_mode; only native 'curved_cut'.

COUNT-MISMATCH: 3 mass elements (cleaver + heel spike + rear_fluke) but only 2 mode_elements. The rear_fluke (3rd mass element + 1 dual_role guard) has NO mode_element — cannot zip 3<->2 by list order. mode_elements[0]=cleaver, [1]=heel spike; rear_fluke silently drops out of both the mode view AND is the sole dual_role catch element.
2 base / 2 mode by list order; the hooked blade (element[0]) is ALSO the sole dual_role catch guard (same x_m 0.6958). whole_gap=0.50 vs point mode gap=0.75 — comparator/strike read whole.
2 base / 2 mode by list order; crescent (element[1]) also the dual_role catch guard at same x_m. whole_gap=0.56 vs point mode gap=0.68.

3 base / 3 mode by list order; no dual_role. whole_gap=0.82; per-mode point gaps 0.57 (beak) and 0.80 (spike) — comparator picks best token by whole-weapon coupling, strike damages with whole 0.82 regardless of which point selected.

3 base / 3 mode by list order; TWO blunt mode_elements (hammer + rear fluke) — element_afforded reads WHOLE-WEAPON percussion_authority(w) for BOTH (per-element percussion is a documented [PHASE-B6 PENDING] gap in the code), so the two blunt modes are indistinguishable to the comparator.

2 base / 2 mode by list order. SHARPEST whole-vs-per-element gap divergence: whole_gap=0.29 but the spike mode_element's own geo['gap']=0.73. When select_mode picks 'point', core.strike still damages with w['gap']=0.29 — under half the spike's true gap-precision. adef_cap('point') also reads w['gap']=0.29.

SILENT-ZIP-FAILURE (design-acknowledged): 3 mass elements (flame forte + tip + ricasso) — one continuous edge, deliberately NO mode_elements. Cited in code as the canonical 'multiple mass elements without multiple modes' case. Extra elements are mass-model + edge_undulation (on element[0]) only; not a selectable-mode bug, but the zip-length divergence (3 vs 0) is real and confirms consumers cannot zip elements<->mode_elements.

SILENT-ZIP-FAILURE + double-count hazard: 2 mass elements (hooked blade + crescent guard-striker) where element[1] IS the dual_role guard (mass carried by element, mass_kg=0 in guard) — the Phase-0 'double-listed under both head_elements and guards' item. NO mode_elements — crescent-strike never a selectable mode; native 'curved_cut' only.

element_mode_inventory_summary.note: 16 of 53 flagged. The linking mechanism between the mass-model `elements` list and the `mode_elements` list is LIST ORDER + COMMENT TEXT only — post-bake mode_elements carry exactly ['geo','head'], no x_m, no extent_m, no element_ref. Confirmed programmatically. 7 weapons have >1 mass element but 0 mode_elements (their extra located parts — including explicit hooks/tines/prongs — never become selectable fight-modes). voulge is the one weapon whose two lists differ in LENGTH (3 vs 2), which by itself proves a naive zip is unsafe.

comparator_table.source_object: WHOLE-WEAPON baked dict (c.w) — NOT the candidate element's geo
comparator_table.note: The comparator ranks each afforded token by core.coupling(...,gap_prec=w['gap']). Every point/blunt candidate is scored on the SAME whole-weapon gap, even for a composite whose selected element has a very different geo['gap'] (goedendag 0.29 vs spike 0.73).
Structurally per-element, but the effectiveness it stores for a 'blunt' token comes from element_afforded's WHOLE-WEAPON percussion_authority(w) (see below).

comparator_table.source_object: MIXED: geo['cut']/geo['gap'] are the PER-ELEMENT geo dict (el['geo']); percussion_authority(w) is WHOLE-WEAPON
comparator_table.note: THE SPLIT-BRAIN FUNCTION. cut/point tokens read the element's own geo; the blunt token reads whole-weapon percussion (code flags this [PHASE-B6 PENDING] at systems.py:268-271). So lucerne_hammer's two blunt mode_elements both read the identical weapon-level percussion — indistinguishable.
For 45 of 53 weapons this returns ONE element carrying the whole-weapon geo — so their 'per-element' path IS the whole-weapon path.

comparator_table.source_object: MIXED: sel_head is the per-beat SELECTED head (Combatant), but gap_prec + perc are WHOLE-WEAPON
comparator_table.note: The damage path pairs a DYNAMIC selected head with a STATIC whole-weapon gap. A bec_de_corbin/goedendag/poleaxe that selects a specific point-element is damaged on w['gap'], not that element's geo['gap'].

comparator_table.source_object: MIXED: head can be the SELECTED head, but w['gap'] is WHOLE-WEAPON
comparator_table.note: Same split as core.strike: point/cut_thrust branches read w['gap'] regardless of which element was selected. Consumed by armor_defeat_sigma + reach_threat.

comparator_table.source_object: Combatant (reads sel_head), delegates gap to adef_cap (whole-weapon)
comparator_table.note: Reads the SELECTED head but the underlying gap comes from adef_cap's w['gap'].

comparator_table.source_object: WHOLE-WEAPON (adef_cap called WITHOUT the selected head -> falls to native w['head'])
comparator_table.note: INCONSISTENCY: armor_defeat_sigma passes sel_head to adef_cap; reach_threat does NOT — so the SAME weapon's armour-defeat capability is scored on the SELECTED head in one place and the NATIVE head in another within the same beat.
Reads the selected damage-mode; only falls to native head when sel_dmg unset.

Gates deflection on the NATIVE head=='point'. A composite whose point-element is SELECTED (sel_head='point') but whose native head is 'blunt'/'cut_thrust' (poleaxe, guisarme, voulge, goedendag) NEVER triggers this branch — the plan's own D5 symptom, confirmed exhaustively: of the 8 mode_element weapons only those with native head 'point' would ever match here, and none of the composites do.

comparator_table.source_object: WHOLE-WEAPON (c.w); reads NEITHER grip_position NOR any element x_m
comparator_table.note: The frozen-er source. Ignores c.grip_position entirely (the plan's dominant symptom). Called live at wrapper.py:32 (frozen into er), :230 (slip_inside, live), and via close_unwieldiness.
gap_thrust gate reads native w['head'] OR the derived afforded_heads set (to catch the poleaxe's emergent point). Consistent with select_mode's affordance, but does not read any gap magnitude.
Guard quantities stay frozen static while reach/damage go dynamic under the plan — the static/dynamic split the coupling lens flagged.

same_key_different_object_flags.note: SAME key name 'gap' read from TWO different objects: element_afforded reads each element's own geo['gap'] to decide AFFORDANCE, but the comparator that RANKS the afforded tokens and the damage/adef paths that RESOLVE the strike read the WHOLE-WEAPON w['gap']. For composites these diverge sharply (goedendag 0.29 vs 0.73; poleaxe spike 0.82 vs whole 0.78; guisarme 0.75 vs 0.50; ji 0.68 vs 0.56; bec_de_corbin spike 0.80 vs whole 0.82; voulge 0.63 vs 0.48). This is exactly the geometry lens's single-instance finding, now enumerated across all 8 mode_element weapons.

readers_whole_weapon: (none in resolution; only via synthesized single-element fallback which IS whole geo)
same_key_different_object_flags.note: geo['cut'] is only ever read per-element in element_afforded (affordance). No competing whole-weapon reader of a differently-scoped 'cut' key in the resolution path, so no cross-object conflict for cut ALONE — but it rides on the same per-element geo dict whose gap sibling IS conflicted.

readers_per_element: (NONE — there is no per-element percussion; this is the documented [PHASE-B6 PENDING] gap)
same_key_different_object_flags.note: The INVERSE conflict: percussion is ONLY ever whole-weapon, so a composite with 2 blunt mode_elements (lucerne_hammer hammer + rear fluke) computes the SAME percussion for both — they are mode-distinct in the schema but percussion-identical in the code. The comparator cannot tell them apart. Coherence problem within one weapon's mode list.

SAME conceptual 'head' resolved from TWO objects: the SELECTED head (Combatant.sel_head/sel_dmg, per-beat) vs the NATIVE head (c.w['head'], static). Split WITHIN one beat: armor_defeat_sigma scores the SELECTED head while reach_threat (same beat, same weapon) scores the NATIVE head via adef_cap. approach_displace + wrapper.py:232 gate on NATIVE 'point', so composites that SELECT a point never trigger the displace/slip-inside logic. Enumerated: all 8 mode_element weapons plus the 7 silent-zip weapons can have sel_head != native head, yet approach_displace/reach_threat/wrapper.py:232 read native.

readers_frozen: reach_sigma (systems.py:409, reads the er dict frozen at wrapper.py:32-33)
same_key_different_object_flags.note: SAME reach quantity read from TWO objects: a frozen-at-engagement-start `er` snapshot vs live per-beat reach_base() calls. Today they agree because reach_base ignores grip; the moment R1's I3 makes reach_base grip-aware, slip_inside/close_unwieldiness see the CHOKED (shorter) reach while er/reach_sigma/longer-shorter still see the FULL reach — a pole could be simultaneously 'longer' (frozen er) and 'shorter' (live reach_base). Extends the coupling lens's 4-claimed-vs-1-actual reach_base cascade finding.

writes: local erA,erB -> er dict (FROZEN for the whole engagement)
beat_call_graph.staleness_risk: er is computed ONCE here and never refreshed; longer/shorter label + measure_gap + closed all derived once. grip_position is not yet set (defaults 0.0). Under R1 grip-aware reach this frozen er is the dominant staleness bug.
none — recomputed each beat from current stamina.
none.
none.
none.

grip_position is written HERE from a LIVE reach_base, but the engagement's er/longer/shorter were frozen at order 0 from the SAME reach_base. Today consistent (grip-blind); under R1 this is the first divergence point — grip_position derives from live reach while the role labels derive from frozen reach.

COMPARATOR reads whole-weapon gap while affordance read per-element geo (the split-brain). Also: select_mode receives `closed` (wrapper.py:69) and IGNORES it (systems.py:302 body never reads closed) — the dead param the plan flags.
reads grip_position set at order 5 — consistent within beat. close_unwieldiness inside close_tempo re-calls reach_base LIVE (again diverging from frozen er under R1).
base_gap from FROZEN er; reach_threat scores adef on NATIVE head while order-6 already selected a possibly-different head. Double staleness (frozen reach + native head).
adef_cap called WITHOUT sel_head -> native head, but sel_head was already set at order 6. If the longer weapon selected a point mode, its armour-defeat is under-scored here.
gates on NATIVE longer.w['head']=='point'. Composites (poleaxe/guisarme/voulge/goedendag) with a SELECTED point never match — the confirmed D5 symptom. sel_head set at order 6 is IGNORED here.
none new (consumes order 9/10 outputs).
core.strike pairs the SELECTED head with WHOLE-weapon gap/perc — the mixed-object damage read, on the approach stop-hit.

MUTATES c.weapon -> c.w now points at a DIFFERENT record. er was frozen on the OLD weapon's reach at order 0; after a half-sword switch the frozen er reach no longer matches the live weapon's reach_base. sel_head set at order 6 was for the OLD form -> re-selected at order 14.
correctly refreshed after the form switch; but er/reach_sigma still use the pre-switch frozen reach.
none; range_avail (R1) would enter here — must not reorder this draw.
none.
grip-aware (reads grip_position from order 5) — internally consistent.

reads the FROZEN er — the standing reach penalty in the CLOSED exchange uses engagement-start reach, NOT live grip-aware reach. Under R1 this is stale vs the grip-aware reach every other consumer this beat used (orders 5,7,10). Confirms + extends the coupling lens: reach_sigma does NOT call reach_base, it reads the frozen snapshot.
none.

writes: local read_win, mode, msig; consumes 1-2 rng draws
beat_call_graph.staleness_risk: legibility reads sel_dmg (dynamic) but mode_sigma reads STATIC baked guard scalars + agility(static MoI). The plan notes agility should read at_grip I_g — today it reads derive(w)['MoI'] (grip-blind), so the defence caps are grip-blind while the offence tempo is grip-aware. Mixed.

armor_defeat_sigma scores SELECTED head but whole-weapon gap; str_demand calls reach_base LIVE (diverges from frozen er used at order 18 same beat). So within ONE net-sigma assembly, reach_sigma used FROZEN reach and str_demand used LIVE reach.
none.
the mixed-object damage read again (selected head + whole gap/perc). This is the primary blow of the beat.
none.

beat_call_graph.function: outcome mapping: strike calls for partial/success/overwhelming
beat_call_graph.staleness_risk: multiple core.strike call sites (core.py graze/success/overwhelming) all inherit the mixed-object gap/perc read.

TWO staleness bugs in one branch: (1) gates on NATIVE aggressor.w['head']=='point' (composites with selected point never trigger); (2) slip_inside calls reach_base LIVE while the longer/shorter identity it implicitly contradicts was frozen at order 0. A pole that choked (order 5) reads shorter live here but is still labeled longer by the frozen er.
uses the FROZEN longer/shorter labels. If reach inverted mid-engagement (R1 grip-aware) these labels are stale, mis-attributing the reopen moment to the wrong fighter.

bind_sigma reads STATIC baked blade_guard (never grip-aware) — consistent with itself but frozen relative to the dynamic reach/damage path. The nested beats+=1 is the structural obstacle to the plan's 'contact phase mirrors bind' claim (bind mutates the loop counter).
role flip is object-safe; but sel_head/sel_dmg/grip_position were computed for the pre-flip roles and are NOT recomputed after the flip within the same beat (next beat re-runs order 5-6).

label_crossref.label: FightInit
label_crossref.note: wrapper.fight() has NO 'FightInit' string/state; the graph node is INFERRED from the fight_start trace event by fired_states_from_events, not read/written by the engine. The engine emits events; the state name exists only in state_graph.py + the trace-mapping test.

label_crossref.label: EngagementInit
label_crossref.note: No 'EngagementInit' symbol in wrapper/systems; inferred from turn_start/engagement_start events.

label_crossref.label: Approach
label_crossref.note: The wrapper has an approach BLOCK (if not closed:) and emits 'approach', but there is no 'Approach' state variable — the node is a trace-inferred label. R1 touches the approach path (stophit/displace) but references no state-graph node by name.

label_crossref.label: AwaitTempo
label_crossref.note: Inferred label for the tempo-gating wait; no engine symbol.

label_crossref.label: Exchange
label_crossref.note: Inferred from commit/read/mode/roll/outcome events; no 'Exchange' symbol in code.

label_crossref.label: Bind
label_crossref.note: The wrapper has a bind BLOCK (if bind:) but no 'Bind' state symbol — it is trace-inferred. R1 decision 8 wires the contact.axis injection point whose node='Bind', and plans grabs gated on bind. So the node is a live-injection-target under R1 but is NOT itself read/written as a named state today.

label_crossref.label: Riposte
label_crossref.note: Trace-inferred from the riposte flag; no state symbol.

label_crossref.label: HitLanded
label_crossref.note: Trace-inferred from hit>0 in the outcome event.

label_crossref.label: Felled
label_crossref.note: The engine has felled LOGIC (Combatant.felled, return shorter/defender) but no 'Felled' state symbol; inferred from engagement_end.felled.

label_crossref.label: Separation
label_crossref.note: The engine emits 'separation' with a reason; the state name is trace-inferred.

label_crossref.label: InterTurn
label_crossref.note: Inferred; the inter-turn stamina/conc recovery lives in fight()'s loop, not a named state.

label_crossref.label: Decided
label_crossref.note: Inferred from a felled winner; no symbol.

label_crossref.label: UpsetCheck
label_crossref.note: The UPSET_FLOOR logic exists in fight() (wrapper.py:332) but no 'UpsetCheck' state symbol; inferred.

label_crossref.label: Unresolved
label_crossref.note: Inferred from winner is None.

label_crossref.label: FinalResult
label_crossref.note: Terminal; inferred from fight_result.

label_crossref.label: INJECTION_POINTS['contact.axis'] (node=Bind, site='(WS-5, unbuilt)')
label_crossref.note: CONFIRMS the geometry lens: there is NO real 'Contact' node in STATES — contact.axis is INJECTION-POINT METADATA only, with site literally '(WS-5, unbuilt)' and generic '(no contact pole today)'. R1 decision 8 promises 'state_graph's documented contact.axis injection point goes live'. It has NO to/emits/terminal wiring; making it live requires ADDING a state, not activating one.

label_crossref.label: INJECTION_POINTS (approach.measure, reopen.measure, exchange.commit, exchange.read, exchange.mode, exchange.bind_entry, exchange.counter, burst.continuation)
label_crossref.note: 8 injection points carry hardcoded wrapper.py line-number `site` strings (e.g. 'wrapper.py:66-74', 'wrapper.py:131-133') but the wrapper does NOT read INJECTION_POINTS — the coupling is a stale comment/line-ref, not a live reference. The IMPOSITION_GATE experiment (impose_node) is the only tradition-injection code, gated off by default (cfg.get('IMPOSITION_GATE')).

label_crossref.label: TRACE_KINDS (fight_start..fight_result, 13 kinds)
label_crossref.note: These ARE live: wrapper._emit(kind,...) fires every one of these kinds (grep-confirmed: fight_start, turn_start, engagement_start, approach, stophit, commit, read, mode, roll, outcome, separation, engagement_end, fight_result). The trace SEAM is the real coupling between wrapper and state_graph, NOT the STATES nodes.

label_crossref.label: SEPARATION_REASONS (collapse, burst_ceiling, clean_defence, beat_exhaustion)
label_crossref.note: All 4 fired by wrapper.engagement() return-None sites (collapse wrapper.py:108,300; burst_ceiling :301; clean_defence :302; beat_exhaustion :303) — grep-confirmed live.

label_crossref.label: capabilities 'halfsword'
label_crossref.note: The GATE PREDICATE (name in HALFSWORD_FORM/BASE) is verified against systems.halfsword_target in the self-test; the wrapper actually CALLS halfsword_target (wrapper.py:120-121). So the capability's UNDERLYING behavior is live, though the wrapper does not call capabilities.allowed() itself — capabilities is a single-source registry the tests bind to, not a runtime gate the wrapper reads.

label_crossref.label: capabilities 'gap_thrust'
label_crossref.note: Predicate (head in point/cut_thrust OR _affords_point via afforded_heads) mirrors the live coupling puncture path + select_mode affordance. Behavior live; the registry is descriptive, tested against the engine, not called at runtime by the wrapper.

label_crossref.label: capabilities 'percussive_blow'
label_crossref.note: Predicate (head=='blunt') mirrors core.HEAD_MODE percussion; live via damage()/select_mode. Same descriptive-registry caveat.

label_crossref.label: capabilities grab/disarm/pin gates
label_crossref.note: R1 decision 8 says 'capabilities.py gains grab/disarm/pin affordance gates'. NONE exist today (only halfsword/gap_thrust/percussive_blow). Planned, not present.

label_crossref_summary.note: ORPHAN COUNT = 13 STATES nodes + 8 INJECTION_POINTS that neither wrapper nor systems references by name = 21. This is the third orphan class the prior lenses did not fully enumerate: NONE of the 15 state_graph STATES is read or written as a named state by the engine — the engine emits TRACE EVENTS and state_graph MAPS events->states post-hoc (fired_states_from_events). The state graph is a DESCRIPTIVE/TEST artifact, not a control structure the engine executes. Only TRACE_KINDS + SEPARATION_REASONS + the 3 capabilities predicates are genuinely live. 'Contact' has no STATE at all (only injection metadata with site '(WS-5, unbuilt)'), confirming the geometry lens exhaustively.

findings.claim: The whole-weapon-gap vs per-element-geo split is roster-wide, not a single instance: element_afforded ranks AFFORDANCE on each element's geo['gap'] but select_mode's comparator (systems.py:326), core.strike (core.py:171), and adef_cap (systems.py:219-220) all RESOLVE on whole-weapon w['gap'].
findings.evidence: All 8 mode_element weapons diverge, measured: goedendag whole 0.29 vs spike geo 0.73 (2.5x); guisarme 0.50 vs 0.75; ji 0.56 vs 0.68; voulge 0.48 vs 0.63; poleaxe 0.78 vs spike 0.82/beak 0.59; bec_de_corbin 0.82 vs beak 0.57; kama_yari 0.70 vs point 0.75. A composite that SELECTS a point-element is damaged/scored on the whole-weapon gap, never that element's own gap.
findings.recommendation: Per the geometry lens: thread the SELECTED element's baked geo['gap'] onto the Combatant (a sel_gap field beside sel_head/sel_dmg) and read it in core.strike/adef_cap/select_mode instead of w['gap']. Do this in I4, not later, since D5 claims to read 'its own geo' while the resolution path reads whole-weapon. Goedendag is the worst case and a good regression anchor.

findings.claim: reach_base ignores grip_position AND the wrapper freezes reach into `er` once per engagement; the frozen er is read by MORE consumers than the coupling lens's 1-of-4, and it coexists in a SINGLE net-sigma assembly with live reach_base reads.
findings.evidence: er frozen at wrapper.py:32-33. Read frozen by: reach_sigma (systems.py:409, order 18), reopen base_gap (wrapper.py:79), longer/shorter label (wrapper.py:37), measure_gap (wrapper.py:38), reopen_moment attribution (wrapper.py:244,247). Read LIVE by: slip_inside (wrapper.py:230), close_unwieldiness/grip_target (systems.py:142, order 5), str_demand (systems.py:82, order 21). Within ONE beat's net-sigma assembly, reach_sigma uses FROZEN reach and str_demand uses LIVE reach. Under R1 grip-aware reach these disagree and the frozen longer/shorter label can contradict live reach (a choked pole 'longer' by er, 'shorter' by live reach_base).
findings.recommendation: Refresh the FULL measure state per-beat (er, then re-derive longer/shorter/measure_gap from refreshed er) in I3, not just er[c]; add an invariant test that within a beat every reach consumer resolves the same grip-aware reach. Decide deliberately whether the longer/shorter LABEL may flip mid-engagement (it drives reopen).

findings.claim: The state graph is a DESCRIPTIVE artifact, not a control structure: 0 of 15 STATES nodes and 8 of 9 INJECTION_POINTS are referenced by name anywhere in wrapper.py/systems.py (21 orphan labels). The engine emits trace events; state_graph maps events->states post-hoc. R1 decision 8's 'contact.axis injection point goes live' is impossible as a drop-in — there is no Contact STATE, only injection metadata with site '(WS-5, unbuilt)'.
findings.evidence: grep confirms no STATES key ('Approach','Exchange','Bind',...) appears as a variable in wrapper/systems; fired_states_from_events(state_graph.py:119-142) reconstructs nodes from the event stream. INJECTION_POINTS carry hardcoded wrapper line-refs as `site` strings but the wrapper never imports/reads INJECTION_POINTS. STATES has no 'Contact' key; contact.axis exists only as INJECTION_POINTS metadata, node='Bind', site='(WS-5, unbuilt)', generic='(no contact pole today)'.
findings.recommendation: Before wiring contact.py (I7), ADD a real Contact STATE to STATES with explicit to/emits edges, and add its trace kind to TRACE_KINDS + a wrapper._emit call — mirroring how the LIVE coupling actually works (events, not named states). Drop the plan's 'contact.axis goes live' framing; it must be BUILT. Also note the bind block mutates the beat counter (beats+=1, wrapper.py:271), so a contact phase cannot cleanly 'mirror bind'.

findings.claim: The SELECTED-head vs NATIVE-head split is inconsistent WITHIN a single beat and across consumers: armor_defeat_sigma scores sel_head while reach_threat (same beat, same weapon) scores the native head via adef_cap called without sel_head; approach_displace and the wrapper's displace/slip gate BOTH gate on native w['head']=='point', so all 8 mode_element composites (+7 silent-zip) that SELECT a point never trigger deflection.
findings.evidence: armor_defeat_sigma passes getattr(aggressor,'sel_head',None) to adef_cap (systems.py:345); reach_threat calls adef_cap(longer.w, cfg) with NO head arg (systems.py:357) -> native. approach_displace reads longer.w['head'] (systems.py:448); wrapper.py:232 reads aggressor.w['head']. None of poleaxe/guisarme/voulge/goedendag/bec_de_corbin/lucerne_hammer have native head 'point' (they are blunt/cut_thrust), so their selected point-mode never activates the displace/slip-inside branch — the plan's D5 symptom, exhaustively confirmed.
findings.recommendation: Route sel_head consistently: pass it to adef_cap in reach_threat, and make approach_displace + wrapper.py:232 read sel_head (falling back to native when unset) as the plan's D5 specifies. Add an equivalence test that at open measure select_mode returns the native head so the approach path stays behavior-preserving until intended.

findings.claim: Percussion is only ever WHOLE-WEAPON, so composites with multiple blunt mode_elements cannot distinguish them, and a choked-up blunt strike deals identical damage to full-extension (the plan's damage-degradation goal fails on the blunt path).
findings.evidence: element_afforded's blunt branch reads WP.percussion_authority(w) whole-weapon (systems.py:281); the comparator (systems.py:326), core.strike (core.py:171), adef_cap (systems.py:217) all whole-weapon. lucerne_hammer has TWO blunt mode_elements (hammer + rear fluke) that both resolve to the identical weapon-level percussion — the schema distinguishes them, the code cannot. percussion_authority(w) has no grip term (weapon_physics.py:224), so a choked poleaxe hammer reads identical percussion to full extension. Code itself flags this [PHASE-B6 PENDING] at systems.py:268-271.
findings.recommendation: Make percussion_authority circumstance-aware in the SAME increment as heft (I2b), and give element_afforded a per-element percussion so the two blunt modes differ. If percussion is deliberately grip-invariant (a hammer's mass doesn't move on the haft), state that and prove the blunt/cut asymmetry is intended so the ablation gate doesn't read it as noise.

findings.claim: 7 multi-element weapons carry located parts (explicit hooks, tines, prongs, wing-lugs, a rear crescent-striker) that NEVER become selectable fight-modes because they lack mode_elements — the select_mode system silently sees only one synthesized whole-weapon mode for them.
findings.evidence: dangpa(3 el), spetum(3), partisan(3), guandao(2), fauchard(2), flamberge(3), hook_sword(2) all have base_element_count>1 but has_mode_elements=false; _mode_elements() (systems.py:254-257) collapses each to [{head=native, geo=whole}]. guandao's rear spike/hook, fauchard's back-hook, hook_sword's crescent striker, the dangpa/spetum/partisan catch-tines are all invisible to mode selection. voulge is worse: it HAS mode_elements (2) but 3 base elements, so its rear_fluke drops out of even the mode view AND is its only dual_role catch element — a 3-vs-2 length mismatch that breaks any zip.
findings.recommendation: Either author mode_elements for the 7 (with explicit x_m/extent so they can be range-gated per D5), or document that these extra elements are mass-model-only by design (as flamberge is). At minimum add the CI/import assertion the schema lens recommends: every mode_element must resolve to a real located element, and flag length mismatches (voulge).

findings.claim: select_mode receives `closed` and ignores it (dead param), so mode selection cannot degrade with measure — the exact hook D5 needs is absent, and the wrapper passes a value the function drops.
findings.evidence: wrapper.py:69 and :123-124 call select_mode(c, opp.armor, closed, cfg); the signature systems.py:302 has `closed` but the body (systems.py:316-335) never reads it. Confirmed by full read. The plan's D5 ('mode selection reads the measure') has no current plumbing — closed reaches the function and is discarded.
findings.recommendation: In I4, actually consume `closed` (and the future measure_gap/range_avail) in select_mode's close-efficacy factor; add a test that at open measure the efficacy factor is ~1.0 so behavior is preserved. Until then D5's per-mode measure term is unbuilt, not merely un-tuned.

findings.claim: agility() is grip-blind (reads derive(w)['MoI'], static) while wield_heft/recoverability_factor/close_tempo are grip-aware (at_grip I_g), so within one beat the defence caps (parry/dodge/wind via defense_affinities->agility) use static inertia while the offence tempo uses grip-aware inertia.
findings.evidence: agility (weapon_physics.py:260) reads derive(w)['MoI']; defense_affinities (weapon_physics.py:278) reads agility -> mode_sigma caps (systems.py:183). wield_heft (systems.py:30) and recoverability_factor (systems.py:125) read WP.at_grip(c.w, grip_position)['I_g']. The plan's I1 calls this out ('agility reads static MoI, not at_grip I_g') and claims byte-identity at g=0 — but the coupling lens shows the I_cm clamp + float round-trip breaks exact identity for the half-sword shifted-origin records.
findings.recommendation: Migrate agility to at_grip I_g in I1 as planned, but VERIFY exact float equality at g=0 across the full roster (especially longsword_halfsword/estoc_halfsword where the I_cm=max(0,I0-m*PoB^2) clamp can fire). If any weapon differs, annotate I1 as a re-baseline, not an identity increment.

findings.claim: reach_threat calls adef_cap WITHOUT the selected head, making the longer weapon's reach-decay use its NATIVE armour-defeat while armor_defeat_sigma (same beat) uses its SELECTED one — a self-inconsistency independent of the gap issue.
findings.evidence: systems.py:357 adef_cap(longer.w, cfg) vs systems.py:345 adef_cap(aggressor.w, cfg, getattr(aggressor,'sel_head',None)). A poleaxe whose select_mode chose its spike (point) has its reach-threat computed on its native blunt max, but its exchange armour-defeat on the spike.
findings.recommendation: Pass the longer fighter's sel_head to adef_cap in reach_threat for consistency, or document that reach-threat is deliberately native-head (a reach advantage is a standing property, not a per-exchange mode). Decide, don't leave it accidental.

findings.claim: The 8 INJECTION_POINTS carry hardcoded wrapper.py line-number `site` strings that the wrapper never reads — a stale-comment coupling that will silently drift as wrapper.py is edited by R1 (which adds facing/range_avail/contact code, shifting every line number).
findings.evidence: INJECTION_POINTS[...]['site'] e.g. 'wrapper.py:66-74','wrapper.py:98-105','wrapper.py:131-133' (state_graph.py:64-90). grep shows wrapper.py does not import INJECTION_POINTS. R1's per-beat facing/range_avail writes + contact branches will move these lines, and nothing tests the site strings against reality.
findings.recommendation: Either drop the line-number site strings (keep only node names) or add a test that each site line-range still contains the described logic. Low severity but a guaranteed R1 drift source.
